data.title: MoneyLion, DailyPay sued by NY Attorney General
data.description:
New York State sued MoneyLion (NYSE:ML) and DailyPay for pushing New Yorkers into short-term expensive payday loans, New York Attorney General Letitia James said on Monday.

The firms took advantage of tens of thousands of New Yorkers with illegal high-interest loans, the AG said. "Both MoneyLion and DailyPay make paycheck advance loans to hourly workers in exchange for fees and tips, pretending to simply be advancing 'earned' wages," the AG's office said in a statement. "Due to the short terms of the loans, the fees MoneyLion and DailyPay charge amount to outrageous annual interest rates in the triple digits, frequently up to 750%."

Furthermore, the office accused both MoneyLion (NYSE:ML) and DailyPay of using "abusive tactics" to push borrowers to take out new loans to cover gaps created by previous loans.

AG James is seeking to end the illegal payday lending practices in New York, obtain restitution for impacted borrowers, and impose civil penalties.

"While many New Yorkers are worried about making ends meet, DailyPay and MoneyLion are making tremendous profits by extracting workers’ hard-earned wages," James said.

In examples the AG's office provided, Daily Pay's most common loan, a seven-day $20 paycheck advance offered for $2.99 reflects an annual interest rate of more than 750%. Meanwhile, more than half of MoneyLion (ML) loans have annual interest rates of more than 500%.

Both MoneyLion (ML) and DailyPay didn't immediately respond to requests for comment.

MoneyLion (ML) stock was roughly flat in early afternoon trading.

data.title: US outlook is still ‘extremely fluid’ says Goldman Sachs
data.description:
Goldman Sachs chief economist Jan Hatzius warns escalating U.S.-China trade tensions and business uncertainty have left the economic outlook “extremely fluid.”

The firm maintains a 45% probability of recession within 12 months.

While telecom and semiconductor tariff exemptions remain in flux, Hatzius notes Chinese exporters might circumvent restrictions through third countries like Vietnam, though broader trade normalization appears unlikely as both nations dig in their heels.

In addition, he highlights surprising labor market resilience despite mounting headwinds, with March employment figures staying solid and jobless claims near historic lows.

However, Hatzius cautions that gross hiring — a critical but harder-to-measure metric — has already slowed pre-tariff and could deteriorate further, potentially pushing unemployment to 4.7% by year-end from 4.2% currently. This uncertain hiring outlook remains a key concern for economists monitoring recession signals, he said.

Inflation dynamics present another puzzle, with core PCE prices rising just 0.08% monthly in March but expected to accelerate to 3.5% annually under current tariff policies. While consumer surveys show rising inflation expectations, market-based measures still align with the Fed’s 2% target — a discrepancy Hatzius attributes to bond market distortions.

He also said Fed officials face “extremely uncertain” policy choices, potentially cutting rates three times as “insurance” against labor market collapse or holding firm if inflation persists.

Also, Goldman’s strategists have turned bearish on the dollar (DXY), calling it 20% overvalued despite recent declines, while equity markets appear to be pricing in only a “baseline” economic scenario. “Current pricing does not offer much risk premium relative to our 45% recession probability,” Hatzius said.

Lastly, Goldman upgraded gold (XAUUSD:CUR) forecasts amid recession fears and sees European bonds attracting safe-haven flows as currency markets price in prolonged U.S. economic underperformance.

data.title: Belgium-based ageas announces €525M accelerated bookbuild offering
data.description:
Belgium-based insurer ageas (OTCPK:AGESY) (OTCPK:AGESF) announced on Monday a €525M accelerated bookbuild offering of newly issued ordinary shares.

The company is set to issue new shares in a private placement to certain institutional and professional investors.

The insurer said the offering will begin immediately, and the proceeds are intended to be used to partly finance the £1.295B esure acquisition.

The company had announced the acquisition of the UK-based personal lines insurer from the private equity firm Bain Capital earlier today.

Shareholders had approved the new stock offering at the extraordinary general meeting held on May 15.

data.title: Sidara bids £242M for Wood Group after offering £1.5B last year
data.description:
John Wood Group (OTCPK:WDGJF) (OTCPK:WDGJY) said United Arab Emirates-based Sidara made an offer to buy the U.K. oil services and engineering firm worth ~£242M ($318M) and a cash injection of as much as $450M, less than a year after walking away from a £1.5B bid for the company.

The 35 pence/share offer, a 40% premium on its 25 pence closing price last week, is ~85% lower than the offer discussed by Sidara last year before it walked away citing "geopolitical risks and financial market uncertainty."

Sidara also said it would take on Wood Group’s (OTCPK:WDGJF) (OTCPK:WDGJY) ~$1.1B of outstanding debt, much of which needs to be refinanced by October.

Wood Group (OTCPK:WDGJF) (OTCPK:WDGJY) said it continued to assess refinancing options after saying in February that it faced another year of cash flow, and "having carefully considered the viability of these options together with its financial advisers, the board of Wood currently believes that the possible offer represents the better option."

Wood Group (OTCPK:WDGJF) (OTCPK:WDGJY) also said it sought an extension of its debt facilities, and that Sidara had made "significant progress" with its due diligence.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow pare gains as Trump spares tech sector from retaliatory tariffs
data.description:
Traders and investors watch the major market averages give up most of its earlier gains on Monday as President Donald Trump announced temporary tariff exemptions on smartphones, computers, and other electronics primarily imported from China.

The tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.3%, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.5%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.3%. 

On a sector-by-sector basis, nine of the 11 S&P sectors have moved up into positive territory with Utilities and Real Estate leading the way. At the other end, Consumer Discretionary has been the weakest performer on the day. 

Shifting to the Treasury market and yields are lower. The longer end U.S. 10 Year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 7 basis points to 4.42%. At the same time, the shorter end U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y) fell 9 basis points to 3.87%. 

Wall Street’s positive momentum push has been fueled by Trump's announcement that tariffs were exempt for smartphones, computers, and other electronics mostly imported from China, providing major relief for American tech players. 

"Tariffs continued to dominate headlines over the weekend, starting with news late on Friday that smartphones, computers, and some other electronics would be exempt from U.S. reciprocal tariffs," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said. 

"Over the weekend, Trump partially retreated from the retreat, emphasizing that a 20% tax would apply and additional unspecified taxes on technology are coming. This erratic policy making leads to investors questioning the existence of a competent plan," UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

The upcoming week is set to feature a wave of major earnings reports from top companies, including Bank of America (BAC), Netflix (NFLX), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), and American Express (AXP).

On the economic front, U.S. consumers have slightly tempered their inflation expectations for the long-term even as they hike their projections on short-term inflation, according to the Federal Reserve Bank of New York's March Survey of Consumer Expectations released on Monday.

Additionally, Atlanta Fed President Raphael Bostic and Richmond Fed President Thomas Barkin make remarks.

As for stocks that are on the move, Palantir pops 4% after landing a deal to supply NATO with AI-powered battle system.

data.title: Apple’s daily volatility is on pace to hit 2008 crisis level
data.description:
Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL), the world’s largest publicly traded company by market capitalization, is currently on track to post a daily move of more than 4%—either up or down—for the fifth consecutive trading session.

This level of sustained volatility has not been observed for the tech stock since the 2008 Great Financial Crisis, underscoring the market’s heightened sensitivity to tech sector dynamics as tariffs continue to shape the market landscape. 

As of Monday afternoon, shares of Apple are trading 4.1% higher, extending a dramatic rebound that has seen the stock surge over 23% in just under a week. This comes after a steep decline of 35% over the previous four months, highlighting the extreme swings investors have had to navigate in recent weeks. 

Apple, a key member of the Magnificent Seven group, is once again at the center of market attention as investors weigh the implications of upcoming earnings, interest rate outlooks, and broader sector rotation.

See the below chart of Apple by Bespoke Investment Group:

 

 

For a more diversified approach towards Apple, investors can also access exposure to the stock through exchange traded funds. Currently AAPL is held by a total of 564 funds and outlined below are the five ETFs with the largest portfolio exposures towards the company. Single stock and leveraged ETFs not included:

No. 5: Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLK), 14.19% allocation in AAPL.

No. 4: iShares U.S. Technology ETF (NYSEARCA:IYW), 15.91% allocation in AAPL.

No. 3: Fidelity MSCI Information Technology Index ETF (NYSEARCA:FTEC), 16.62% allocation in AAPL.

No. 2: Vanguard Information Technology ETF (NYSEARCA:VGT), 17.89% allocation in AAPL.

No. 1: iShares Global Tech ETF (NYSEARCA:IXN), 17.99% allocation in AAPL.

data.title: Senators call on Trump administration to get rid of AI Diffusion rule
data.description:
A group of Republican senators have called on the Trump administration to remove the so-called “AI Diffusion Rule” laid out by the Biden administration on the grounds that it could impact the U.S.'s leadership in artificial intelligence. 

“We applaud President Trump’s commitment to ensuring American dominance in the tech sector,” the letter, sent to Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick, read. ”Today, we are in an enviable position: American companies dominate in crucial areas that will define tomorrow’s economy including semiconductor design, compute infrastructure, and artificial intelligence (AI). This leadership position has been hard fought. Maintaining and growing our tech lead requires diligently advancing an American-led, global ecosystem around the world.”

“With the compliance deadline of May 15, 2025, rapidly approaching, immediate action is necessary to prevent irreversible damage to American innovation and competitiveness,” the letter continued. “Every day this rule remains in place, American companies face mounting uncertainty, stalled investments, and the risk of losing critical global partnerships that cannot be easily regained. Therefore, we urge you to withdraw this rule and propose an alternative that is effective in preventing Communist China from capturing the world market in a leading technology without compromising American advantages.”

The letter was written by senators Thom Tillis (R-NC), Markwayne Mullin (R-OK), Ted Budd (R-NC), Roger Wicker (R-MS), Eric Schmitt (R-MO), and Tommy Tuberville (R-AL).

The AI Diffusion Rule, which was enacted in early January, was designed to limit the spread of advanced AI technology outside the U.S., with the purpose to prevent foreign threats from accessing it. It puts countries into three tiers based on their relationship with the U.S. and limits the number of chips that can go to these countries.

A number of companies and organizations have spoken out against the AI Diffusion Rule, including Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA), which called it “unprecedented and misguided” on the grounds that it could “derail innovation and economic growth worldwide.”

The AI Diffusion Rule, which some analysts have said could be negatives for companies like Nvidia and Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO), is set to go into effect on May 15.

data.title: Newmark announces integration of property, facilities management service lines
data.description:
Commercial real estate advisor Newmark Group (NASDAQ:NMRK) announced on Monday the unification of its property management and facilities management service lines.

"We expect our clients to benefit from the synergies between Property and Facilities Management, while also providing owners and occupiers enhanced access to a broader suite of integrated solutions," said COO Luis Alvarado.

data.title: Lightspeed, Flywire cut to Underweight at J.P. Morgan as tariffs prompt wary payments outlook
data.description:
J.P. Morgan's North America Equity Research team downgraded Flywire Corp. (NASDAQ:FLYW) and Lightspeed Commerce Inc. (NYSE:LSPD) shares to Underweight from Neutral as tariffs are driving uncertainty and prompting a cautious economic outlook.

On Flywire (NASDAQ:FLYW), its "stock has been battered by adverse student immigration policy, but trends likely won’t improve near-term assuming U.S. visa flow could be directly and indirectly impacted by trade war headlines as retaliation and inflation make studying abroad incrementally less attractive and weigh on demand for FLYW’s largest business line," analyst Tien-tsin Huang wrote in a note.

Shares of the payments enablement and software firm have dropped nearly 60% so far this year, and ~62% from a year earlier. The SA Quant system rating views FLYW a Strong Sell, as opposed to the average Wall Street analyst rating of Buy.

Commerce platform Lightspeed (NYSE:LSPD), meanwhile, faces potential risks to growth estimates in the second half of the year, given a "higher mix of discretionary spend exposure, including complex retail stores (e.g., bike shops) subject to tariff uncertainty." The analyst also pointed to "ongoing execution risk of restructuring on the back of [the] strategic review conclusion."

Both FLYW and LSPD were little changed in Monday afternoon trading.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Deutsche upgrades EHang on entry point after selloff
data.description:
• Deutsche Bank upgraded EHang Holdings (NASDAQ:EH) to Buy from Hold with a price target of $20, down from $22. The firm cites valuation for the upgrade following the stock's recent selloff.
  • China appears to be in the early innings of cultivating a new electric vertical take-off and landing aircraft/urban air mobility ecosystem, the analyst tells investors in a research note.
  • The firm believes EHang "represents an intriguing first step," having been awarded the Civil Aviation Administration of China's maiden regulatory approvals for an eVTOL and now on track to scale up production to fulfill a large backlog that includes overseas orders.
  • Deutsche sees an attractive entry point at current share levels.
  • EH is +7.4% to $15.51.
